content.text:
Looking at the current YC categorization logic in the file, I can see it's checking for patterns like `yc[fsw][0-9]{2}` (lines 129-131). The code `ycx26` follows a similar pattern but with 'x' instead of 'f', 's', or 'w'. 

Let me update the regex pattern to include 'x' as a valid character:

input.old_string:
-- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fsw][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
input.new_string:
-- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_prospect_to_org_journey
PURPOSE: Combined view of prospects and organizations with promo code categorization
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a unified view of the customer journey from prospect to organization,
including all records from both mysql_prospects and mysql_organizations tables.
It applies promo code categorization logic directly to prospects and includes
all columns from both tables (prospect columns prefixed with prospect_, org columns prefixed with org_).

GRANULARITY:
One row per combined prospect/organization record, including:
- Matched prospect-organization relationships (prospect and org columns populated). When a prospect has multiple organizations, the prospect data will be duplicated across multiple rows (one per organization).
- Prospects without any related organization (all org_* columns NULL).
- Organizations without any related prospect (all prospect_* columns NULL).

IMPORTANT - DUPLICATE WARNING:
This view can contain duplicate prospect_id values when a single prospect is associated 
with multiple organizations. Always use COUNT(DISTINCT prospect_id) when counting unique 
prospects to avoid inflated counts. The org_stripeid values are unique (no duplicates).

As of 2026-02-11, the distribution of prospect-to-organization relationships:
- 27,245 prospects have 1 organization (normal case)
- 55 prospects have 2 organizations
- 5 prospects have 3 organizations
- 1 prospect has 7 organizations (extreme edge case)

KEY COLUMNS:
- prospect_* columns: All columns from mysql_prospects table prefixed with prospect_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- org_* columns: All columns from mysql_organizations table prefixed with org_ (Airbyte columns excluded)
- promo_code_type: Categorized promo code type (YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners), Customer Referral, Marketing (No Promo Code), Marketing (Discount Code), No Related Prospect, or NULL)
  - NULL/empty prospects get 'Marketing (No Promo Code)' category
  - Specific marketing codes get 'Marketing (Discount Code)' category
  - Codes ending in 'stref' get 'Customer Referral' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
  - Uncategorized promo codes get NULL
- utm_type: Categorized UTM medium type (Marketing (Organic), Marketing (Paid), or No Related Prospect)
  - NULL/empty medium gets 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Paid channels (cpc, ppc, paidsocial, paid, newsletter, display, cpc\\, podcast) get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
  - Organic channels (organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com) get 'Marketing (Organic)' category
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category
- marketing_attribution: Final marketing attribution combining promo code and UTM data
  - YC, Stripe, Referral (Other Partners) keep their promo_code_type values
  - Marketing categories and Customer Referral use their utm_type values
  - Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect' category

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.mysql_prospects: Source table with prospect data
- public.mysql_organizations: Source table with organization data

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-06
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new
DISTKEY(prospect_id)
SORTKEY(prospect_createdat, org_createdat)
AS
WITH base_data AS (
    SELECT 
    -- Prospect columns (prefixed with prospect_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    p.id AS prospect_id,
    p.plan AS prospect_plan,
    p.email AS prospect_email,
    p.gclid AS prospect_gclid,
    p.notes AS prospect_notes,
    p.state AS prospect_state,
    p.fbclid AS prospect_fbclid,
    p.medium AS prospect_medium,
    p.source AS prospect_source,
    p.keyword AS prospect_keyword,
    p.campaign AS prospect_campaign,
    p.interval AS prospect_interval,
    p.lastname AS prospect_lastname,
    p.referral AS prospect_referral,
    p.adgroupid AS prospect_adgroupid,
    p.createdat AS prospect_createdat,
    p.firstname AS prospect_firstname,
    p.partnerid AS prospect_partnerid,
    p.promocode AS prospect_promocode,
    p.updatedat AS prospect_updatedat,
    p.campaignid AS prospect_campaignid,
    p.gaclientid AS prospect_gaclientid,
    p.companyname AS prospect_companyname,
    p.initialstate AS prospect_initialstate,
    p.locationcode AS prospect_locationcode,
    p.referrerhost AS prospect_referrerhost,
    p.referrerpath AS prospect_referrerpath,
    p.stablesource AS prospect_stablesource,
    p.promocodename AS prospect_promocodename,
    p.qualification AS prospect_qualification,
    p.referrersearch AS prospect_referrersearch,
    p.landingpagehost AS prospect_landingpagehost,
    p.landingpagepath AS prospect_landingpagepath,
    p.registeredagents AS prospect_registeredagents,
    p.stripecustomerid AS prospect_stripecustomerid,
    p.clearbitpersonkey AS prospect_clearbitpersonkey,
    p.landingpagesearch AS prospect_landingpagesearch,
    p.clearbitcompanykey AS prospect_clearbitcompanykey,
    p.initiallocationcode AS prospect_initiallocationcode,
    p.failedpaymentmethodhold AS prospect_failedpaymentmethodhold,
    
    -- Organization columns (prefixed with org_, excluding Airbyte columns)
    o.id AS org_id,
    o.name AS org_name,
    o.hasbaa AS org_hasbaa,
    o.nickname AS org_nickname,
    o.stripeid AS org_stripeid,
    o.createdat AS org_createdat,
    o.updatedat AS org_updatedat,
    o.prospectid AS org_prospectid,
    o.outboundwebhookid AS org_outboundwebhookid,
    
    -- Promo code categorization logic
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- YC codes get 'YC' category
                WHEN p.promocodename = 'yc'
                    OR p.promocodename = 'ycombinator'
                    OR p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fsw][0-9]{2}$'
                THEN 'YC'
                
                -- Stripe Atlas codes get 'Stripe' category
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ '^stripe(atlas|partner|atlaspartner)$'
                THEN 'Stripe'
                
                -- Other referral partner codes get 'Referral (Other Partners)' category
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                    'mercury',
                    'levy',
                    'chore',
                    'doola',
                    'central',
                    'pocketbook',
                    'blinkdigital',
                    'producthunt50',
                    'codi50',
                    'remofirst50',
                    'clerkydeal',
                    'openphone',
                    'arightco',
                    'rocketlawyerf50',
                    'mosey',
                    'speedinvest',
                    'vera',
                    'f6s',
                    'casium',
                    'gatewayx',
                    'northone',
                    'northoneplus',
                    'everyio',
                    'goprint',
                    'seamess',
                    'flex',
                    'justworks',
                    'cvconcepts',
                    'moxe',
                    'kmg',
                    'fptrial50',
                    'founderpass50grow',
                    'briars',
                    'thetaxvalet',
                    'pilot',
                    'cfoadvisors',
                    'ledgerly',
                    'cfoproject',
                    'burkland',
                    'buildingprofits',
                    'inventus',
                    'inventuslaw',
                    'wsgr',
                    'gunderson',
                    'cytlaw',
                    'epigram',
                    'ashbury',
                    'flowfi',
                    'fusion',
                    'deel',
                    'brex',
                    'pulley',
                    'clerky',
                    'hustlefund',
                    'sus',
                    '500startups',
                    'skydeck',
                    'ondeck',
                    'acceleprise',
                    'villageglobal',
                    'aircfo',
                    'magicbell',
                    'southparkcommons',
                    'levro',
                    'talentdrop',
                    'producthunt',
                    'tacostars',
                    'lombardstreet',
                    'fondo',
                    'stablefam',
                    'precursor20',
                    'precursor50'
                ) THEN 'Referral (Other Partners)'
                
                -- Customer Referral codes (ending in 'stref')
                WHEN p.promocodename ~ 'stref$' THEN 'Customer Referral'
                
                -- Marketing (No Promo Code) - NULL or empty promo codes
                WHEN COALESCE(p.promocodename, '') = '' THEN 'Marketing (No Promo Code)'
                
                -- Marketing (Discount Code) - specific marketing campaign codes
                WHEN p.promocodename IN (
                        '100creator2025',
                        '3monthsfree',
                        '50pros',
                        'abstractops',
                        'allthehacks',
                        'business50',
                        'cooley',
                        'customplan',
                        'efficient',
                        'emsoft',
                        'eoy25',
                        'fall50',
                        'florida',
                        'formation',
                        'gdsvfh',
                        'https://dashboard.usestable.com/onboard/begin?',
                        'katie50',
                        'kelly50',
                        'killb',
                        'novo',
                        'ph50',
                        'pod',
                        'redesignhealth',
                        'remote',
                        'scale20',
                        'scale50',
                        'stableil2024',
                        'stablema2024',
                        'stablewy2024',
                        'swap20',
                        'texas',
                        'tldr50',
                        'worklife'
                    ) THEN 'Marketing (Discount Code)'
                
                -- Everything else gets NULL (uncategorized/new codes)
                ELSE NULL
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS promo_code_type,
    
    -- UTM Type categorization based on medium field
    CASE 
        -- Organizations without prospects get 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN p.id IS NULL AND o.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        -- Apply categorization for prospects
        WHEN p.id IS NOT NULL THEN
            CASE 
                -- Paid channels get 'Marketing (Paid)' category
                WHEN p.medium IN (
                    'cpc',
                    'ppc', 
                    'paidsocial',
                    'paid',
                    'newsletter',
                    'display',
                    'cpc\\',
                    'podcast'
                ) THEN 'Marketing (Paid)'
                
                -- Everything else (including NULL/empty and organic channels) gets 'Marketing (Organic)'
                -- Explicitly listed organic channels: organic, social, email, referral, post, description, card, getro.com
                ELSE 'Marketing (Organic)'
            END
        -- This shouldn't happen in a FULL OUTER JOIN, but keeping for completeness
        ELSE NULL
    END AS utm_type

    FROM public.mysql_prospects p
    FULL OUTER JOIN public.mysql_organizations o
        ON p.id = o.prospectid
)
SELECT 
    *,
    -- Marketing Attribution - combines promo_code_type and utm_type logic
    CASE 
        WHEN promo_code_type = 'No Related Prospect' THEN 'No Related Prospect'
        WHEN promo_code_type IN ('YC', 'Stripe', 'Referral (Other Partners)') THEN promo_code_type
        -- For all other cases (Marketing categories and Customer Referral), use utm_type
        ELSE utm_type
    END AS marketing_attribution
FROM base_data;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old table and rename new table
BEGIN;
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_prospect_to_org_journey;
    ALTER TABLE view_prospect_to_org_journey_new RENAME TO view_prospect_to_org_journey;
COMMIT;

input.content:
# YCX26 Promo Code Categorization Update

## What We Changed

Updated the YC promo code categorization logic in `views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql` to include promo codes with the pattern `ycx[0-9]{2}`.

### Specific Change
- **File**: `views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql`
- **Line**: 131
- **Before**: `p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fsw][0-9]{2}$'`
- **After**: `p.promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'`

This regex pattern now matches YC promo codes in these formats:
- `ycf##` (e.g., ycf24)
- `ycs##` (e.g., ycs24)
- `ycw##` (e.g., ycw24)
- `ycx##` (e.g., ycx26) - **NEW**

## How to Test

### 1. Rebuild the View
First, execute the SQL file to rebuild the view with the updated logic:
```sql
-- Run the entire script to recreate the view
\i views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql
```

### 2. Verify YCX26 Categorization
Check that prospects with the `ycx26` promo code are now categorized as 'YC':
```sql
SELECT 
    prospect_id,
    prospect_promocodename,
    promo_code_type,
    marketing_attribution
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey
WHERE prospect_promocodename = 'ycx26';
```

Expected result: `promo_code_type` and `marketing_attribution` should both show 'YC'.

### 3. Verify All YCX Pattern Codes
Check for any other YCX pattern promo codes that should now be categorized:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
    prospect_promocodename,
    promo_code_type,
    COUNT(*) as count
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey
WHERE prospect_promocodename ~ '^ycx[0-9]{2}$'
GROUP BY prospect_promocodename, promo_code_type
ORDER BY prospect_promocodename;
```

### 4. Confirm No Regression
Verify that existing YC categorizations still work:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
    prospect_promocodename,
    promo_code_type
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey
WHERE prospect_promocodename ~ '^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$'
    OR prospect_promocodename IN ('yc', 'ycombinator')
ORDER BY prospect_promocodename;
```

All results should show `promo_code_type = 'YC'`.

### 5. Check for Uncategorized YC-like Codes
Look for any remaining uncategorized codes that might follow similar patterns:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
    prospect_promocodename
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey
WHERE prospect_promocodename ~ '^yc'
    AND promo_code_type IS NULL
ORDER BY prospect_promocodename;
```

If this returns any results, they may need to be added to the categorization logic.

## Impact

This change ensures that YC promo codes following the `ycx##` pattern (like `ycx26`) are properly categorized as 'YC' in our prospect-to-organization journey view, improving the accuracy of our marketing attribution reporting.

input.content:
# YCX26 Promo Code Update

## Change
Updated YC promo code regex pattern in `views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql` (line 131):
- From: `^yc[fsw][0-9]{2}$`
- To: `^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$`

Now categorizes `ycx26` and similar patterns as 'YC'.

## Test
```sql
-- Rebuild view
\i views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql

-- Verify ycx26 is categorized as 'YC'
SELECT prospect_promocodename, promo_code_type
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey
WHERE prospect_promocodename = 'ycx26';
```

# YCX26 Promo Code Update

## Change
Updated YC promo code regex pattern in `views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql` (line 131):
- From: `^yc[fsw][0-9]{2}$`
- To: `^yc[fswx][0-9]{2}$`

Now categorizes `ycx26` and similar patterns as 'YC'.

## Test
```sql
-- Rebuild view
\i views/standard_data_refresh/20_prospect_to_org_journey.sql

-- Verify ycx26 is categorized as 'YC'
SELECT prospect_promocodename, promo_code_type
FROM view_prospect_to_org_journey

```
